LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION NOTICE OF MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING WRIT OF MANDATE; ARGUED, DENY MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. NO NEW FACTS OR LAW. PURSUANT TO CCP 128(A)(8), COURT AMENDS PARAGRAPH 4 OF THE 4/9/02 ORDER GRANTING THE AGRICULTURAL PETITIONER'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. THE ORDER SHOULD STATE THAT "ENVIRONMENTAL DEFENSE CENTER'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS MOOT." WHETHER OR NOT EDC'S CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF ARE MOOT OR IMPROPERLY SEEK ADVISORY OPINIONS NEEDS TO BE RESOLVED AT A LATER PROCEEDING. THE PRIOR HEARING ONLY PERTAINED TO THE PETITIONS FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND THE COURT DID NOT ADJUDICATE THE VIABILITY OF THE OTHER CLAIMS. PLTF TO PREP ORDER. REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN #8189

Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Feb-21-2002 Continued To Apr-10-02 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: APPEAR: Steve Velyvis, Esq. & Victoria Clark, Esq. 805/963-1622 for petitioners Environmental Defense Center, Environmental Working Group, Pesticide Action Network, Physicians for Social Responsibility-Los Angeles, Friends of the Earth, Environmental Center of San Luis Obispo; Michael Meuter, Esq. 831/757-5221 & Cynthia Rice, Esq. 510/835-2478 for petitioner Roberto Solorio; Robert P. Roy, Esq. 805/388-2727 & Glenn J. Dickinson, Esq. 805/485-1000 for petitioners Ventura County Agricultural Association, DB Specialty Farms, Emerald Mist Berry Farms, LLC, Ramon Zepeda, D.W. Berry Farms, Inc.; Anita E. Ruud, Deputy Atty General 703-5533 for deft California Department of Pesticide Regulation; William J. Thomas, Esq. & Craig Lundgren, Esq. 916/442-1111 for Intervenors Agricultural Interests and Alliance of the Methyl Bromide Industry.
RULING: Argued and the Court grants writ on ground that there was failure to consult Food & Agriculture, stays order granting writ and voiding regulations to allow Dept. of Pesticide to consider implementing those regulations on an emergency basis. Court declines to reach the other issues on ground that they are moot. All parties stipulate to Court's continuing jurisdiction. Court sets status conference re status of matter for 04/10/02 at 3:30pm. Mr. Dickinson to prepare proposed order and writ. Stayed 30 days.
